figures.caption: Fig. 1. MonoEM-GS constructs a map in which each Gaussian aggregates measurements over time. Despite contradictory point-cloud predictions at timestamps t and t+1, the Gaussians remain consistent, with only a single update step needed to incorporate new observations.
Fig. 6. Reconstructed Gaussians (upper row) and corresponding meshes (bottom row) created from the Gaussian centers and normals.
Fig. 7. MonoEM-GS Gaussian centers colored by their predicted classes (right) and the nearest ground-truth points colored by their GT classes (left).

Fig. 2. Single iteration of processing a new input image by MonoEM-GS. For each new RGB input, MapAnything [27] produces a dense point cloud prediction oNt . We align it with the new prediction for frame N-1: oN−1
Fig. 3. Views of the current prediction alignment with the map. View 1: misaligned current prediction oNt ; View 2: predictions oN−1

Fig. 4. Multi-domain scene reconstructions produced by MonoEM-GS. A: Gaussian Splatting created by the proposed approach; B: Mesh reconstructed from Gaussian centers and normals; C: DINOv3 [29] feature map; D: Open-set 3D semantic map.
